meaning: vt.表现,描绘,声称,象征,扮演,回忆,再赠送,再上演vi.提出异议
examples: The carvings represent a hunting scene.
A lawyer was appointed to represent the child.
What do the shaded areas on the map represent?
Negative briefing documents from FDA staff hardly represent a death knell for a drug.
The provisions, part of the 650-page corporate tax bill enacted in October, represent an about-face by Congress.
